page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, it features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The server operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gb.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place this instance in the top 10% of tested systems, supported by high-speed memory bandwidth exceeding 480 GB/sec for uncached reads and 218 GB/sec for uncached writes. It also achieves top-tier results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls into the strong to average ranges, highlighting its strength in highly parallelized workloads.

The ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded applications, avoiding the resource contention of hyperthreaded environments. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and parallel data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of memory, a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top 10% placement in multi-threaded benchmarks, memory bandwidth tests, and database operations. Its dedicated core design provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing by eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The c8gb.24xlarge is optimized for highly parallel workloads, including software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
